what_it_does: Defines a 24 by 24 SVG outline icon of an unlocked padlock using a rounded rectangle body and an open shackle path.
observations: The SVG uses a 24x24 viewBox with explicit width and height of 24.
It has no fill and uses currentColor for the stroke.
Stroke styling is set to width 1.5 with rounded caps and joins.
The lock body is a rounded rectangle positioned at x=5, y=11, width=14, height=10, rx=2.
The shackle is a path rising from the left side of the body and arcing open across the top.
standouts: Single compact SVG with only two drawing elements.
Color is inherited through currentColor for easy theming.
conventions: Follows common outline icon conventions with fill="none", stroke="currentColor", and rounded stroke ends.
Stores the full icon definition inline in one SVG element.
notes: This file is a small themeable SVG asset representing an unlocked lock state.
questions: Is the one-line SVG formatting intentional for the asset pipeline?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename indicates an unlock icon, and the SVG content matches that purpose by drawing an open padlock.
intention_signals: It seems intended to provide a reusable unlocked-state icon that can inherit color from surrounding UI text or controls.
contribution: Contributes a compact, currentColor-based outline unlock symbol that a fused system would keep as a reusable security or access-state icon asset.
summary_for_embedding: A 24x24 currentColor SVG outline icon showing an unlocked padlock with a rounded rectangular body and open shackle.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for representing an unlocked access state as a reusable, themeable SVG icon.
